dairy-free-gluten-free-chocolate-chip-co.name: Dairy-free, Gluten-free Chocolate Chip Cookies
instructions: Preheat the oven to 163°C (165 degrees C). Line 2 baking sheets with parchment paper.
Whisk together flour, baking soda, and salt.
Beat brown sugar and vegan butter in a large bowl for 2 to 3 minutes. Mix in eggs and vanilla. Add flour mixture and mix until combined. Fold in vegan chocolate chips.
Drop small spoonfuls of dough 2 inches apart onto the prepared baking sheets.
Bake in the preheated oven until edges are golden, rotating halfway through about 10 minutes. Cool on the baking sheet briefly before removing to a wire rack to cool completely.

delicious-nuggets-burrito-step-by-step-r.name: Delicious Nuggets Burrito - Step By Step Recipe
delicious-nuggets-burrito-step-by-step-r.original_name: Delicioso Burrito de Nuggets - Receta paso a paso

ingredients.item: 4  tortillas grandes de harina de trigo
1/2 taza de tomates cherry cortados por la mitad

instructions: It purifies the oven as instructed by the nuggets package and cooks nuggets following them. Once ready, let them cool down for a few minutes.
Warm tortillas in a frying pan or microwave for a few seconds to make them flexible and easy to roll up.
In a small bowl, mix mayonnaise and tomato sauce. Reserve this gravy for burrito.
Coloca una tortilla en una superficie plana y extiende una cucharada de la salsa de mayonesa y tomate en el centro de la tortilla.
Coloca 2 nuggets de pollo en la tortilla, seguidos de la lechuga picada y los tomates cherry.
Season with salt and pepper to taste and then bend the sides of the omelette inwards and roll from the bottom, making sure to keep the ingredients inside the burrito.
Repite el proceso con las tortillas y los ingredientes restantes.
If you wish, you can heat up burritos wrapped up in a slightly frying frying pan for a few minutes so they are hot and golden.
Serve nuggets burritos cut in half and join them with mayonnaise and remaining tomato sauce.
Enjoy your delicious nuggets burrito!

delicious-oatmeal-and-orange-cookies-com.name: Delicious Oatmeal And Orange Cookies
delicious-oatmeal-and-orange-cookies-com.original_name: Deliciosas galletas de avena y naranja fáciles
instructions: It purifies the oven at 180 ° C and prepares a paper plate. The cookies are expanding, so leave room with them.
Bat butter with brown sugar and get a creamy mixture. You don't have to have her whitened, but she's just a uniform and with no butter clumps.
Add an egg, a vanilla if you use it, an orange frying and a juice. Mix up and integrate and the mass should be smooth and with clear citrus flavour.
Combines oatmeal, flour, baking powder and salt. Break the bigger flakes with your hands if you want a more thin cookie and if you leave them whole, you have more texture.
It incorporates dry ingredients into the wet mixture. The mass should be thick and manageable without being dry. If the notes are too compact, add an extra tablespoon of orange juice.
It forms portions of the size of a tablespoon. Put them on the plate and clap them slightly with the fingers or back of a spoon.
Hornea 12-15 minutes. You will know that they are ready as the edges become golden and the centre still looks soft and will tighten as they cool.
Let them rest for 5 minutes and put them onto a grid. If hot moves can be broken, as the oatmeal takes a while to set up the structure.

dill-pickled-vegetables.name: Dill-pickled Vegetables

ingredients.prep: white, and/or yellow pearl onions (about 2 cups)

instructions: In a large saucepan of boiling water blanch onions 3 minutes and drain in a colander. Cool onions just until they can be handled and peel. Arrange onions and carrots in two 1-quart Mason-type jars.

In pan bring vinegar, water, sugar, dill seeds, and salt to a boil and simmer 3 minutes. Pour hot mixture over onions and carrots and cool completely. Add celery. Chill vegetables, covered, at least 12 hours to develop flavors. Pickled vegetables may be made up to 3 weeks ahead but do not add celery until 1 day before serving.

double-chocolate-zucchini-bread.name: Double-chocolate Zucchini Bread

ingredients.item: . diamond crystal or 1 0.25 tsp. morton kosher salt

ingredients.prep: grated on the large holes of a box grater

instructions: Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to 325°. Coat a 9x5" loaf pan with nonstick vegetable oil spray and line with parchment paper, leaving generous overhang on long sides. If using, toast 1 cup (115 g) walnuts on a small rimmed baking sheet, toasting halfway through, until golden brown, 8–10 minutes. Let cool, then coarsely chop.

Whisk 2 large eggs, 1 cup (packed; 200 g) light brown sugar, ¾ cup vegetable oil, ½ cup (32 g) Dutch-process cocoa powder, 2 tsp. Diamond Crystal or 1¼ tsp. Morton kosher salt, 2 tsp. vanilla extract, and 1 tsp. ground cinnamon in a large bowl until smooth, about 2 minutes. Vigorously whisk in ½ tsp. baking powder and ½ tsp. baking soda.

Add 1 lb. zucchini (about 4 small), scrubbed, grated on the large holes of a box grater, to batter and stir to combine. Add 1½ cups (188 g) all-purpose flour, 1 cup (190 g) semisweet chocolate chips, and walnuts (if using) and stir again just until no large dry streaks of flour remain. Scrape batter into prepared pan. Sprinkle 2 Tbsp. raw or granulated sugar evenly over top.

Bake zucchini bread, rotating pan front to back halfway through, until a tester inserted into the center comes out clean (an instant-read thermometer inserted into the center should registers 200°), 60–80 minutes. Transfer pan to a wire rack and let bread cool in pan. Run a small offset spatula or knife around sides of bread to help loosen, then turn out onto a platter.
Do ahead: Bread can be baked 2 days ahead. Store uncut bread loosely covered at room temperature. Once sliced, store airtight and chill.

double-fried-chicken-wings.name: Double-fried Chicken Wings
instructions: Season chicken wings with equal parts salt and pepper—just enough to lightly coat the wings, then rub gently into wings.
Mix flour and cornstarch in a mixing bowl. Dredge each wing in the mix until there’s NO visible moisture on each wing. Shake off excess, then set aside on a plate or rack.
Pour 2 inches of oil in a pot, or enough to submerge the wings. (If you’re frying up whole wings—where the drumette, the wingette, and the tip haven’t been cut apart—pour in 3 inches.)

Over high heat, heat the oil to 177°C. Par(tially)-fry the wings for 10 minutes, then remove from pot, and shake off any excess oil. The par-fry cooks the chicken completely through, sealing in the flavor, while starting to form the outside crispy layer.
Set wings on a paper towel–lined plate or rack for 5 minutes, or until they cool to room temperature. This prevents the meat from overcooking, while keeping in its moisture.
OPTIONAL: You can store the par-fried wings in the fridge and do the second wing fry the next day. Just be sure the wings are at room temperature when you fry them.

Once the wings cool down, reheat the oil to 177°C and fry them a second time for 10 more minutes. The second fry crisps the wings to the point of delicious crunchiness. Remove wings, then shake off excess oil. Brush on or toss wings in a bowl with your favorite sauce. Or, if you just want the purest form of savory, crunchy wings around, eat ’em as is, which I often do as a “taste test.”

dreamsicle-iced-tea-latte.name: Dreamsicle Iced Tea Latte
instructions: Combine 1 cup water and brown sugar in a saucepan; bring to a boil, stirring frequently. Remove from heat; stir in almond extract and vanilla extract.
Combine remaining water and almond milk in a saucepan; bring to a boil. Add tea bags; steep for 5 minutes. Remove tea bags; stir in extract mixture. Fill two tall glasses with ice; pour in tea.

2. Mix flour, baking powder, carbonate, salt and cake in a bowl.

dry-martini-argiro.name: Dry Martini

ingredients.item: 10-15 mlextra dry or simple dry white dry vermouth
Green olives passed on bamboo stick
Lemon peel or lime for garnishing
10-15 mlextra dry or simple dry white dry vermouth
Green olives passed on bamboo stick
Lemon peel or lime for garnishing

instructions: The recipe for dry martini cocktails consists of two main ingredients, gin (longon dry type) or vodka and dry vermouth in proportions differentiated according to consumer preferences.
A typical dry martini contains 70 ml London dry Gin or Vodka and a few drops or 10 ml of very dry vermouth type extra dry.
Mix the ingredients of the recipe with a barspoon in a glass stirring container or even better metal for 30 seconds.
Strain in a cold Martini glass (cocktail glass).
A second way of executing the recipe is by hitting a Shaker filled with lots of ice all ingredients for 20 seconds. It's good if we prefer to enjoy our martini with gin, avoid using the Shaker.
Garnish with green olives that we pin on a bamboo stick, or alternatively with a lemon peel or lime, after spraying its oils over our cocktail.

Depending on the consumer's preferences, with regard to the content of gin or dry vermouth, there are the following versions. For all versions we follow the same way of execution as described in the classic recipe.
Extra dry Martini 70 ml London dry gin or vodka 5 ml extra dry vermouth 1 drop aromatic orange bitters (optional) olives green or lemon peel or lime for garnishing
Wet Martini 50 ml London dry gin or Vodka 50 ml white dry vermouth type extra dry 1 drop aromatic orange bitters (optional) olives green or lemon peel or lime for garnishing

Perfect Martini 60 ml London dry gin or Vodka 10 ml white dry vermouth type extra dry or dry 10 ml sweet vermouth white or red 1 drop aromatic orange bitters (optional) olives green or lemon peel or lime for garnishing
Upside down Martini 60 ml white dry vermouth type extra dry or dry 40 ml London dry gin or Vodka 1 drop aromatic orange bitters (optional) olives green or lemon peel or lime for garnishing
Dirty Martini 70 ml London dry gin or Vodka 10-15 ml extra dry or simple dry white dry dry Vermouth 10 ml green olives (optional) green olives passed on bamboo stick for garnishing

duchess-baked-potatoes.name: Duchess Baked Potatoes
instructions: Place potatoes in a large pot and pour in water to cover by 2"; season with salt. Bring to a boil over medium-high. Reduce heat and simmer until a paring knife very easily slides through potatoes, 25–35 minutes. Drain and let cool slightly.

Preheat oven to 425°. Whisk egg yolks, garlic, cream, sour cream, butter, and peppercorns in a large bowl; season generously with salt. Peel potatoes and pass flesh through a ricer or a food mill (or mash them by hand if you don’t mind a few lumps) directly into bowl with egg mixture. Fold in gently (be careful not to overmix). Transfer to a 3-quart baking dish and decoratively shingle or swirl surface. Bake, rotating once, until golden brown and slightly puffed, 30–40 minutes.
Do Ahead: Dish can be assembled 1 day ahead. Cover and chill. Increase bake time by 5–10 minutes.

dukkah-roasted-potatoes.name: Dukkah Roasted Potatoes
instructions: Preheat the oven to 191°C (190 degrees C).
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, 1/4 teaspoon salt, and black pepper together in a small bowl.
Place potatoes and garlic cloves in an 11x13-inch baking dish; drizzle with olive oil mixture and toss to coat.
Bake in the preheated oven for 40 minutes, stirring every 10 to 15 minutes.
Combine butter and dukkah in a small bowl; brush on potatoes. Continue baking until potatoes are soft and golden, 10 to 15 minutes more. Season with salt; garnish with parsley.
